content.problemStatement:
Do you know Professor Saeed? He is the algorithms professor at Damascus University. Yesterday, he gave his students hard homework (he is known for being so evil) - for a given binary string SSS, they should compute the sum of F(S,L,R)F(S, L, R)F(S,L,R) over all pairs of integers (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1≤L≤R≤∣S∣1 \le L \le R \le |S|1≤L≤R≤∣S∣), where the function F(S,L,R)F(S, L, R)F(S,L,R) is defined as follows:


Create a string UUU: first, set U=SU = SU=S, and for each iii (L≤i≤RL \le i \le RL≤i≤R), flip the iii-th character of UUU (change '1' to '0' or '0' to '1').
Then, F(S,L,R)F(S, L, R)F(S,L,R) is the number of valid pairs (i,i+1)(i, i + 1)(i,i+1) such that Ui=Ui+1U_i = U_{i+1}Ui​=Ui+1​.
As usual, Professor Saeed will give more points to efficient solutions. Please help the students solve this homework.
constraints: the sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 6⋅1066 \cdot 10^66⋅106
the sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 600600600
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ∑1≤L≤R≤∣S∣F(S,L,R)\sum_{1 \le L \le R \le |S|} F(S, L, R)∑1≤L≤R≤∣S∣​F(S,L,R).
